questions.question: How many disease cubes of each color are in the game?
questions.expected_answer: There are 24 disease cubes of each color (96 total).

questions.question: How many cards does each player draw at the end of their turn?
questions.expected_answer: Each player draws 2 Player cards at the end of their turn.

questions.question: What happens when the player card deck runs out?
questions.expected_answer: The players immediately lose the game if the player card deck runs out.

questions.question: How many actions can a player take on their turn?
questions.expected_answer: A player can take up to 4 actions on their turn.

questions.question: What is an outbreak and what triggers one?
questions.expected_answer: An outbreak occurs when a city already has 3 disease cubes of one color and would receive another. The cube is not placed; instead, a cube is placed in each connected city.

questions.question: How do players win the game?
questions.expected_answer: Players win by discovering cures for all 4 diseases.

questions.question: How many Epidemic cards are shuffled into the player deck in the standard game?
questions.expected_answer: 4 Epidemic cards are used in the introductory game, 5 in the standard game, and 6 in the heroic game.

questions.question: What is the Dispatcher role's special ability?
questions.expected_answer: The Dispatcher may move any pawn to a city containing another pawn, and may move another player's pawn as if it were their own.

questions.question: When does a quarantine specialist prevent disease placement?
questions.expected_answer: The Quarantine Specialist prevents disease cube placement and outbreaks in the city they are in and all cities connected to it.

questions.question: How many research stations can be on the board at one time?
questions.expected_answer: There can be at most 6 research stations on the board at one time.
